senior_junior_culture.summary: No strong, independently repeated evidence was found to support a definitive claim. One comparison thread reports that ragging was not a problem, while official sources show elected student bodies and formal student-support structures. Treat senior-junior culture as a limited anecdotal signal rather than a verified campus-wide fact.
positive_themes: peer guidance is available in some student discussions
formal SAC, guidance and anti-ragging structures exist officially
negative_themes: experiences may differ by hostel, batch and branch

academic_culture.summary: Official sources describe an autonomous credit-based curriculum with laboratories, computing and library infrastructure. Student discussions generally characterize academics and infrastructure as adequate to strong, but the source sample is too small for claims about strictness, grading pressure or faculty quality by department.
negative_themes: workload/strictness varies and was not established
academic_culture.evidence_strength: Moderate official evidence; limited student-reported evidence

hostel_reality.summary: The institute officially reports 15 hostels with dining, common/reading rooms and internet. Visual tours and discussions indicate meaningful variation between old and newer blocks; some rooms are described as basic or congested. Mess experience is subjective and may vary by hostel.
positive_themes: multiple hostels and attached dining halls
visual evidence of functioning hostel spaces
negative_themes: room size and maintenance vary by block
some older rooms described as average/congested
mess quality is inconsistent across personal reports
hostel_reality.evidence_strength: Strong official facility evidence; moderate repeated qualitative signal for block-to-block variation

club_and_campus_life.summary: Official sources list a broad range of technical, cultural, service and sports clubs, and the institute runs Tathva and Ragam. Visual sources consistently show a large, green residential campus. Activity levels should still be rechecked club by club.
negative_themes: some club pages lack recent dated activity
club_and_campus_life.evidence_strength: Strong official signal; moderate visual/student-reported support

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: The official presence of CPHUB, FOSSCell, AI Club, RIG and CCD training supports an active technical-preparation ecosystem. Student reports are mixed on how strong coding culture feels outside computing-focused peer groups. Official NIRF outcomes show solid overall placements, but branch-specific placement rates and eligibility are not available in the verified dataset.
positive_themes: coding programs and hackathons from CCD
negative_themes: student perception of coding culture varies by branch and peer group
coding_and_placement_culture.evidence_strength: Strong official infrastructure/outcome signal; conflicting student-reported culture signal

locality_and_student_life.summary: The campus is scenic and residential but approximately 22 km from Kozhikode city. Official sources report frequent local buses and road access; qualitative reports suggest late-night city travel may be less convenient than daytime travel.
positive_themes: on-campus banks, post office, health care and shops
negative_themes: late-night public transport may be limited
locality_and_student_life.evidence_strength: Strong official distance/connectivity evidence; limited anecdotal late-night signal

overall_satisfaction.summary: Available evidence points to a generally positive academic and residential-campus proposition, with the strongest recurring cautions being hostel-block variation, distance from the city and uneven branch-specific perceptions of coding/placement culture. The qualitative source base is not large enough to assign a numeric satisfaction rating.
